content: NIST AI RMF calls for defined human-AI roles, documented capability scope and knowledge limits, production monitoring, human oversight, appeal/override, incident response and recovery. The NIST playbook and OWASP agent guidance add histories/audit logs, least privilege, action previews, risk-based autonomy boundaries, structured validation, bounded retries and the ability to interrupt or roll back. GOLC should therefore keep model interpretation separate from deterministic validated commands and make manual blackout/release irrevocably higher priority.
